description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with a boxy, rectangular form and a predominantly off-white or light gray casing. The front features a large, slightly recessed display area with a black screen and bright green horizontal lines, reminiscent of early monochrome CRT monitors. The bezel surrounding the screen is thick and flat, matching the color of the main body, and has sharp, angular corners. Both sides of the monitor have horizontal ventilation slats, suggesting internal cooling for electronic components. The rear is plain and solid, with no visible ports or controls, while the base is slightly extended, providing stability. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for use in urban or industrial environments.
description: A boxy, retro computer monitor with a thick off-white bezel, black screen with green lines, and side ventilation slats.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a solid, featureless off-white panel with no visible ports or controls.
description_view_1: The left side view displays horizontal ventilation slats and the monitor's rectangular depth.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the black screen with green lines, framed by a thick off-white bezel.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with matching horizontal ventilation slats.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and off-white, with sharp, angular corners and a smooth matte finish. It is visually plain with no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor has a wide, flat base integrated into the lower part of the casing, providing stability. The base is rectangular and matches the main body in color and material, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
